'''Sylvester Namo''' (born 26 August 2000) is a Papua New Guinean professional [[rugby league]] footballer, who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for the [[Brisbane Tigers]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].

He represents [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at international level and played at the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 Rugby League World Cup]]. He has previously played for the [[Papua New Guinea Hunters|PNG Hunters]] and [[Townsville Blackhawks]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. He has spent time on [[RFL dual registration|dual registration]] from Castleford at [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

==Background==
Born in [[Goroka]], [[Papua New Guinea]], Namo began playing rugby league for the KK Brothers Vikings in 2019 at [[Mount Hagen|Mt. Hagen]] where he was raised.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://postcourier.com.pg/double-debut-for-namo/|title=Double Debut for Namo|date=8 February 2021|website=Post Courier}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Lae Snax Tigers===
In 2020, Namo made his debut for the [[Lae Snax Tigers]] in the [[Papua New Guinea National Rugby League|Digicel Cup]], scoring a try in their Grand Final loss to the [[Hela Wigmen]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.thenational.com.pg/tepend-looking-to-recruit-players-from-nines-tourney/|title=Tepend looking to recruit players from nines tourney|date=17 December 2020|website=The National}}</ref>
===PNG Hunters===
In 2021, Namo joined the [[PNG Hunters]], playing 13 games for the club in the [[Queensland Cup]]. In 2022, he played 16 games, starting all 16 at prop.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://pnghausbung.com/meet-the-png-kumuls-debutants-sylvester-namo/|title=Meet the PNG Kumuls Debutant - Sylvester Namo|date=25 June 2022|website=PNG Haus Bung}}</ref>

===North Queensland Cowboys===
In November 2022, Namo joined the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] on a train and replacement contract for the 2023 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.thenational.com.pg/cowboys-to-trial-namo/|title=Cowboys to trial Namo|date=21 November 2022|website=The National}}</ref>
===Castleford Tigers===
On 1 October 2023, it was announced that Namo would join the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-year contract from [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=1 October 2023 |title=Sylvester Namo: Castleford sign PNG international as Liam Watts agrees new deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66976792 |access-date=25 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=1 October 2023 |title=Namo is a Tiger |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8780 |access-date=1 October 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He made his debut on 8 March against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]],<ref>{{Cite web |date=8 March 2024 |title=Super League: Castleford 8-50 Huddersfield - Tigers suffer fourth straight loss to nine-try Giants |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68501494 |access-date=25 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> with coach [[Craig Lingard]] managing his minutes as he returned from an [[Anterior cruciate ligament injury|ACL injury]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=4 April 2024 |title=Sylvester Namo returns for Castleford Tigers as coach Craig Lingard provides injury latest |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/sylvester-namo-returns-castleford-tigers-as-head-coach-craig-lingard-provides-injury-latest |access-date=25 May 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> In April, Namo was given a five-match ban for dangerous contact against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], with Castleford unsuccessful in their appeal against the ruling.<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 April 2024 |title=Sylvester Namo: Castleford Tigers lose appeal against five-game ban |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68809496 |access-date=25 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=18 April 2024 |title=Revealed: Why Sylvester Namo was found guilty |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/revealed-why-sylvester-namo-was-found-guilty-and-why-its-nonsense-90444/ |access-date=25 May 2024 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> He made his return to the team on 24 May against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], earning praise from Lingard for an impactful performance.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=24 May 2024 |title=Craig Lingard explains Castleford Tigers' win over Hull FC, an almost messy toilet situation and why Cain Robb didn't play |url=https://www.totalrl.com/craig-lingard-explains-castleford-tigers-win-over-hull-fc-an-almost-messy-toilet-situation-and-why-cain-robb-didnt-play/ |access-date=25 May 2024 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

On 23 April 2025, Castleford announced that Namo had left the club with immediate effect.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx204gk3wkmo|title=Slvester Namo: Castleford Tigers release Papua New Guinea forward|date=23 April 2025|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=23 April 2025}}</ref>

===Brisbane Tigers===
On 5 May 2025 it was reported that he had joined the [[Brisbane Tigers]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 May 2025|title=Castleford Tigers departee finds new club Down Under following release|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-departee-finds-new-club-following-release|author=Louis Chapman Coombe}}</ref>

===International===
On 25 June 2022, he made his international debut for [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in their 24-14 victory over [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] in the 2022 Pacific Test.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Papua New Guinea v Fiji |url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/internationals/2022/round-1/papua-new-guinea-v-fiji/ |access-date=2022-06-28 |website=National Rugby League |language=en}}</ref> In October 2022, he represented Papua New Guinea at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]], playing three games.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/five-stars-world-cup-move-25351857|title=Five World Cup Stars Who Have Caught the Eye|date=25 October 2022|website=Examiner Live}}</ref>

On 12 October 2025 he played for the [[PNG Prime Minister's XIII]] in the 28-10 defeat to Australia’s [[Prime Minister's XIII]] in Port Moresby<ref>{{cite web|website=FoxSports.au|date=12 Oct 2025|author=Daniel Nuttall|title=Zero for seven?! Aussie’s double hat-trick powers PM’s XII past PNG despite horrific kicking|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/rugby-league-2025-aus-pm-xiii-v-png-pm-xiii-teams-where-to-watch-start-time-kevin-walters-mitch-moses-rugby-league-news/news-story/b7b5454aadeb76356a021e7e9bbcefa3}}</ref>
